suggested_topics: why some bridges last 2000 years and others collapse in 20
how coral reefs build their own weather systems
the logistics of running a 24-hour diner
why birds migrate in V formations — physics not instinct
the hidden math behind musical scales

formats.name: imagined_future
formats.instruction: Write a single paragraph describing an unusual future scenario. Paint a vivid mental picture using sensory details and one surprising twist.

formats.name: rapid_fire_truths
formats.instruction: List 5 ultra-short statements about a topic—each must reveal something honest, unexpected, or controversial. No extra explanation.

formats.name: reverse_advice
formats.instruction: Share a bad habit or mistake you made related to your topic. Reflect on why you stuck with it and what it taught you, ending with a question for readers.

formats.name: mystery_box
formats.instruction: Present three clues about an object, concept, or event. Don't say what it is—let readers guess. Clues should be enigmatic but honest.

formats.name: docudrama_summary
formats.instruction: Write a dramatic summary of a real-world event as if it were the plot of a movie. Use suspenseful language and invent dialog from key figures.

formats.name: one_sentence_manifesto
formats.instruction: Declare your personal philosophy or belief about a topic in a single bold sentence. Make it thought-provoking and evocative.

formats.name: interview_with_myself
formats.instruction: Create a Q&A session where you ask and answer your own deep, weird questions on the topic. Write at least three exchanges.

formats.name: lost_instruction_manual
formats.instruction: Pretend you found a mysterious instruction manual for something ordinary. Write the first section as cryptic steps, blending real advice with surreal details.

formats.name: scavenger_hunt
formats.instruction: Offer 3-5 concrete challenges or things to discover in the reader's day, each connected to your topic. Make each step playful and unexpected.

formats.name: deep_map
formats.instruction: Construct a metaphorical map for navigating a complex idea. Name 3-5 'regions', describe their features and dangers. Use creative names.

title_styles: Begin with a metaphor or image that links to your topic: 'How gardening taught me about procrastination'
Use an overheard phrase or soundbite as your title: 'I hear the future is up for grabs'
Write your title as a dare or challenge: 'Convince me that sleep isn't underrated'
Frame your title as a confession: 'Why I secretly love blank spaces'
Make your title a riddle: 'What do snowflakes and passwords have in common?'
structure_variants: Start with a vivid anecdote, then break down your insights in two parts.
Present your main point, then immediately counter it with the opposite argument.
Use a numbered list of metaphors, each illuminating your topic from a new angle.
Write as a dialogue between two imagined characters who debate your idea.
Organize content in a series of unanswered questions, each opening a new door.
topic_angles: Hidden rituals people perform before big decisions
Unexpected lessons found in abandoned places
How humor changes the seriousness of a crisis
What your childhood collection says about your adult personality
